modules.gui.dialog.addto_zarr module#

class AddToZarrDialog(parent: QWidget, series: Series)[source]#

Bases: QDialog

accept()[source]#

Overwritten from QDialog.

addInput()[source]#

Add a group input.

exec()[source]#

Run the dialog.

removeInput()[source]#

Remove a group input.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("AddToZarrDialog" inherits "QDialog": )#
updateButtons()[source]#

Show/hide buttons according to number of inputs.

modules.gui.dialog.alignment module#

class AlignmentDialog(parent: QWidget, alignment_names: list, current_alignment: str)[source]#

Bases: QDialog

accept()[source]#

Overwritten from parent class.

exec()[source]#

Run the dialog.

newAlignment()[source]#

Add a new alignment to the list.

removeAlignments()[source]#

Remove selected alignments from the list.

renameAlignment()[source]#

Rename a selected alignment.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("AlignmentDialog" inherits "QDialog": )#
class AlignmentList(parent: QWidget, alignment_names: list, current_alignment: str)[source]#

Bases: QTableWidget

addAlignment(alignment: str)[source]#

Add an alignment to the list.

Params:

alignment (str): the name for the new alignment

createTable()[source]#

Create the table.

getSelectedAlignments() → list[str][source]#

Get the name of the objects highlighted by the user.

Returns:

(list): the name of the objects

removeAlignment(alignment: str)[source]#

Remove an alignment from the list.

Params:

alignment(str): the name of the alignment to remove

renameAlignment(alignment: str, new_name: str)[source]#

Rename an alignment on the list.

Params:

alignment (str): the alignment to rename new_name (str): the new name for the alignment

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("AlignmentList" inherits "QTableWidget": )#

modules.gui.dialog.all_options module#

class AllOptionsDialog(parent, series: Series)[source]#

Bases: QDialog

accept()[source]#

Overwritten–called when OK is pressed

addOptionWidget(name: str, structure: list, setOption, grid=False)[source]#

Add a widget to the all_widgets dictionary

Params:

name (str): the desired name for the widget structure (list): the QuickDialog structure for the widget setOption (function): the function for setting the widget option once accepted grid (bool): True if widget structure should be a grid instead of rows

createWidgets(use_defaults=False)[source]#

Create the widgets for each of the options

Params:

use_defaults (bool): True if all values should be set at defaults instead of current settings

getWidgetsLayout(structure: list)[source]#

Create a layout from a predifined widget structure

(Ideally the structure for a full tab)

Params:

structure (list): the desired layout structure

placeWidgets()[source]#
resetDefaults()[source]#

Reset the default values for the widget inputs

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("AllOptionsDialog" inherits "QDialog": )#
class OptionWidget(parent, title: str, structure: list, series: Series, setOption, grid=False)[source]#

Bases: QuickDialog

paintEvent(self, event: PySide6.QtGui.QPaintEvent) → None[source]#
set()[source]#

Set the series option for the widget

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("OptionWidget" inherits "QuickDialog": )#

class ColorButton(color: tuple, parent)[source]#

Bases: QPushButton

getColor()[source]#
resizeEvent(self, event: PySide6.QtGui.QResizeEvent) → None[source]#
selectColor()[source]#

Called when button is clicked: prompts user to change color

setColor(color)[source]#

Sets the visual color for the button.

class FlagDialog(parent: QWidget, flag: Flag)[source]#

Bases: QDialog

createRow(comment, index)[source]#

Create a row of the dialog.

exec()[source]#

Run the dialog.

removeRow(index)[source]#

Remove a row from the dialog.

resizeNewComment()[source]#

Resize the new comment field to its contents.

resizeToContents()[source]#

Adjust the size of the dialog to fit its contents

modules.gui.dialog.quick_dialog module#

class InputField(type, widget, check_params=None, required=False)[source]#

Bases: object

getResponse()[source]#
class QuickDialog(parent, structure: list, title: str, grid=False, include_confirm=True)[source]#

Bases: QDialog

accept()[source]#

Overwritten from parent class.

exec()[source]#

Run the dialog.

get(structure: list, title='Dialog', grid=False)[source]#

Create a quick dialog and return the inputs.

Params:

parent (QWidget): the parent of the input dialog structure (list): the structure of the input dialog title (str): the title of the dialog grid (bool): True if grid layout

getLayout(structure: list, grid: bool)[source]#

Return the layout for a given structure.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("QuickDialog" inherits "QDialog": )#
class QuickTabDialog(parent, structures: dict, title='Dialog', grid=False, scrollable=False)[source]#

Bases: QuickDialog

accept()[source]#

Overwritten from parent class.

get(structures: list, title='Dialog', grid=False)[source]#

Create a quick dialog with tabs and return the inputs.

Params:

parent (QWidget): the parent of the input dialog structures (list): the structure of the input dialog title (str): the title of the dialog grid (bool): True if grid layout

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("QuickTabDialog" inherits "QuickDialog": )#

modules.gui.dialog.shapes module#

class ShapesDialog(parent: QWidget)[source]#

Bases: QDialog

exec()[source]#

Run the dialog.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("ShapesDialog" inherits "QDialog": )#
resizeShape(points: list)[source]#

Modify a set of points so that they have a radius of 1 centered about the origin.

Params:

points (list): the set of points describing the shape

shapeToIcon(points: list, size=30)[source]#

Convert a trace to an icon.

Params:

points (list): the list of points describing the shape size (int): the side length of the icon
